cases.title: WHERE with scope - no DB-wide columns, only scope tables

expected.comment: Scope=[users]. No scope column starts with 'p'. DB-wide columns excluded. Only functions.

cases.title: WHERE with JOIN scope - only scope table columns
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id=o.user_id WHERE s|

expected.comment: Scope=[users as u, orders as o]. Scope columns: u.status, o.status. DB-wide excluded.

cases.title: WHERE with CURRENT_TABLE not in scope - CURRENT_TABLE ignored

expected.comment: Scope=[orders]. CURRENT_TABLE=users not in scope. Only scope column orders.user_id.

cases.title: WHERE with CURRENT_TABLE in scope - CURRENT_TABLE included

expected.comment: Scope=[users]. CURRENT_TABLE=users in scope. Table-name expansion: users.* in schema order.
